rag_slow.name: RAG Slowdown
rag_slow.description: Retrieval latency spike. Students should find slow RAG span in trace.
rag_slow.symptom: High response latency (2-3s per request) with most of the time spent in the RAG retrieval span
rag_slow.root_cause: rag_slow incident flag is enabled in the system, introducing an artificial 2.5 second delay in RAG retrieval
rag_slow.expected_effect: p95 latency SLO violation, slow chat responses, visible in /metrics and traces
detection_hints: Look at span durations in traces, specifically the retrieval span
Check if rag_slow is enabled in /health endpoint or incidents.json

tool_fail.name: Vector Store Failure
tool_fail.description: Vector store or tool error. Students should identify error_type from logs.
tool_fail.symptom: 500 Internal Server Error responses from /chat endpoint with error type 'RuntimeError'
tool_fail.root_cause: tool_fail incident flag is enabled, causing the RAG retrieval function to throw 'Vector store timeout' exception
tool_fail.expected_effect: High error rate visible in /metrics, failed requests in logs with error_type
detection_hints: Look for 'request_failed' log events with error_type=RuntimeError
Check if tool_fail is enabled in /health endpoint

cost_spike.name: Cost Spike
cost_spike.description: Token usage spike. Students should explain higher output token cost.
cost_spike.symptom: Unusually high token usage and cost per request, with output tokens 4x higher than normal
cost_spike.root_cause: cost_spike incident flag is enabled, multiplying output token count by 4, increasing cost
cost_spike.expected_effect: High token count and cost visible in /metrics and logs
detection_hints: Check token and cost metrics in /metrics
Look for 'response_sent' log events with high tokens_out values
Check if cost_spike is enabled in /health endpoint
